title: Best Time to Buy and Sell Stock
description: You are given an array prices where prices[i] is the price of a given stock on the ith day. You want to maximize your profit by choosing a single day to buy one stock and choosing a different day in the future to sell that stock. Return the maximum profit you can achieve from this transaction. If you cannot achieve any profit, return 0.
thinking_questions: You must buy before sell. At day i, the best you could have bought is the minimum price seen so far — how do you track that?
At each day, best profit = today's price - minimum price seen so far. One pass?
What if prices only decrease? What should you return?

hints.L1: Scan forward tracking the lowest price seen — best profit at any day is today's price minus that minimum.
hints.L2: One pass: maintain min_price. At each day compute profit=price-min_price, update best, then update min_price.
hints.L3:
# INSIGHT: at each day, optimal buy = global min of all previous prices
min_price = prices[0]
best_profit = 0
for price in prices:
    best_profit = max(best_profit, price - min_price)
    min_price = min(min_price, price)
return best_profit
# Time: O(n)  Space: O(1)

follow_up_questions: What if you could make unlimited transactions? (LC 122)
What if you could make at most 2 transactions? (LC 123) — how does state change?
Why is this considered a sliding window problem? What is the window?
solution_notes: Track running min_price, compute profit each step. O(n), O(1). Base case for stock problem series.
